report_name: Pro-Iranian Hacker Group Handala Claims Cyberattack on California Water Infrastructure, Leaks Stolen Data

threat_actors.name: Handala
threat_actors.description: A pro-Iranian hacktivist group that claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against California Water Service. The group framed the intrusion as a retaliatory 'warning', exfiltrated 5GB of data including PII and credentials, but stated it deliberately refrained from disrupting water services.
threat_actors.goals: To send a retaliatory 'warning' to the U.S. for alleged strikes on Iranian water resources through a cyberattack on U.S. critical infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The likely initial access vector was the exploitation of a vulnerable, internet-exposed component of an RTKBase NTRIP GPS correction network.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: After the initial breach, the attackers used stolen administrative credentials to access and navigate a network management interface, gaining broad administrative access.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: The group collected and exfiltrated 5GB of data, including customer PII and administrative credentials, from a billing system or connected database.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The attackers exfiltrated 5GB of stolen data to their own servers before leaking it publicly.

identities.name: California Water Service
identities.description: A large U.S. water utility serving two million customers, identified as the victim of the Handala cyberattack. Its Chico, Bakersfield, Salinas, and Stockton districts were affected by the breach.

infrastructure.name: Compromised RTKBase GPS Network
infrastructure.description: An internet-facing RTKBase NTRIP GPS correction network that was compromised and likely used as the initial entry point for the attackers to pivot and steal credentials.

infrastructure.name: Handala Data Leak Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used by the Handala group to host and publicly leak the 5GB of data stolen from California Water Service.

indicators.name: Suspicious RTKBase or NTRIP URL Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious RTKBase or NTRIP URL Access
id: 9b1d8c4a-7e6f-4b5a-8d3c-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects unusual access patterns or exploit attempts against NTRIP/RTKBase servers, which are often used in utilities and may be internet-facing.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-13
references:
  - https://www.isssource.com/iran-linked-attack-group-claims-ca-water-utility-breach/
level: medium
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative access or scanning.
tags:
  - attack.t1190
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - '/rtkbase/'
      - '/ntrip/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects web requests containing URL patterns associated with RTKBase or NTRIP servers, which may indicate reconnaissance or exploitation attempts against these systems.

indicators.name: Anomalous Large Data Egress from Internal Database
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Large Data Egress from Internal Database
id: a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6e
status: experimental
description: Detects large data transfers from internal database servers to external endpoints, which could indicate data exfiltration.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-13
references:
  - https://www.isssource.com/iran-linked-attack-group-claims-ca-water-utility-breach/
level: medium
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate large data exports, backups, or replication.
tags:
  - attack.t1567
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    src_ip:
      # Add internal database server IPs here
      - '10.0.10.0/24'
      - '192.168.50.12'
    action: 'allow'
    # This requires a backend that can track bytes transferred
    bytes_out|gt: 5000000000 # 5GB
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects large outbound data transfers (e.g., >5GB) from internal database servers, which could be indicative of data exfiltration as seen in this attack.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious RTKBase or NTRIP URL Access
Anomalous Large Data Egress from Internal Database
